3. G ratuity
CHAPTER 261
PRESIDENTIAL EMOLUMENTS Act
2 of 1981
30 of 1985
An Act to repeal and replace the Presidential Emoluments Act; to provide for the 21 of 1987
revised salary, allowance and gratuity payable to the holder of the office of 35 of 1988
President; and to provide for matters connected with or incidental to the 20 of 1989
foregoing. 3 of 1991
30 of 1992
45 of 1994
[6th March, 1981]
26 of 1996
18 of 1997

sections.heading: (1) This Act may be cited as the Presidential Emoluments Act. Short title
sections.text: 1. (1) This Act may be cited as the Presidential Emoluments Act. Short title

sections.heading: The President shall be paid- Salary and allowance
sections.text:
2. The President shall be paid- Salary and allowance
(a) a salary calculated at the annual rate of K6,161,963; and
(b) a special annual allowance of K4,018,269.
(As amended by Act No. 18 of 1997)
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: A person who has held the office of President for at least three years shall, upon Gratuity
sections.text:
3. A person who has held the office of President for at least three years shall, upon Gratuity
the expiration of his term of office or upon his ceasing to be President, be paid a gratuity
equal to twenty-five per centum of the aggregate amount of emoluments paid or payable
to him in respect of the period spent in office:
Provided that where a person who holds office of President ceases to hold office-
(a) by reason of his death; or
(b) when a new President assumes office after an election following the
dissolution of Parliament before its full term;
that person shall be deemed to have served as President for the specified period of three
years for the purpose of this section.
(As amended by Act No. 3 of 1991)
